records.ety: [Pref. pre + judge: cf. F. prjuger. Cf. Prejudicate, Prejudice.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Prejudged (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Prejudging.]

defs: To judge before hearing, or before full and sufficient examination; to decide or sentence by anticipation; to condemn beforehand.
quotes: The committee of council hath prejudged the whole case, by calling the united sense of both houses of Parliament“ a universal clamor.”
